root_text.sn4.11:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.sn4.11:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā rattandhakāratimisāyaṁ abbhokāse nisinno hoti, devo ca ekamekaṁ phusāyati.
root_text.sn4.11:1.3: Atha kho māro pāpimā bhagavato bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato avidūre mahante pāsāṇe padālesi.
root_text.sn4.11:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā “māro ayaṁ pāpimā” iti viditvā māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn4.11:4.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ bhagavā, jānāti maṁ sugato”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn4.11:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.sn4.11:1.2: Now at that time the Buddha was meditating in the open during the dark of night, while a gentle rain drizzled down.
translation_text.sn4.11:1.3: Then Māra the Wicked, wanting to make the Buddha feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, approached him, and crushed some large boulders close by him.
translation_text.sn4.11:2.1: Then the Buddha, knowing that this was Māra the Wicked, addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn4.11:4.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The Buddha knows me! The Holy One knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.
